navigation_system.purpose: Comprehensive traffic obfuscation and DPI bypass for DAP Stream protocol

metadata.title: DAP Stream Traffic Obfuscation & DPI Bypass Implementation
metadata.description: Implement traffic obfuscation and DPI bypass mechanisms for DAP Stream protocol with alternative transport layers (UDP, WebSocket, etc.) to ensure secure communications in restrictive network environments

problem_statement.current_situation: DAP Stream protocol is tightly coupled to HTTP transport, making it vulnerable to DPI (Deep Packet Inspection) detection and blocking in restrictive network environments
pain_points: All DAP Stream traffic currently uses HTTP, making it easily identifiable
No traffic obfuscation mechanisms in place
No fake traffic injection to mask VPN patterns
Initialization and encryption handshake are HTTP-only
VPN client may not work with new protocols
business_impact: Services can be blocked by DPI systems in restrictive regions
VPN traffic patterns are easily detected and blocked
No protection against traffic analysis and fingerprinting
Limited deployment in censored regions (China, Iran, Russia, etc.)
Potential revenue loss from blocked markets
User privacy compromised by detectable patterns

objectives.primary: Implement comprehensive traffic obfuscation and DPI bypass for DAP Stream protocol with support for multiple transport layers
secondary: Implement alternative transports (UDP, WebSocket, custom protocols)
Inject artificial traffic to mask VPN patterns
Update VPN client for backward and forward compatibility
success_criteria: DPI systems cannot detect DAP Stream traffic
Traffic patterns do not resemble VPN
VPN client works with old and new protocols
Code follows DAP SDK coding standards

issues: Session creation expects HTTP query strings

process: Client sends enc_type, pkey_exchange_type via HTTP query
Server receives base64-encoded public key in HTTP body
Server generates session key, stores in session
Server returns encrypted session key via JSON response

issues: No mechanism to select alternative transports

udp_support.status: Basic UDP support exists but not integrated with encryption handshake

vpn_client.integration: Tightly coupled to current HTTP-based protocol

modern_techniques.name: Traffic Fragmentation
modern_techniques.description: Split packets into smaller fragments to confuse DPI pattern matching

modern_techniques.name: Protocol Obfuscation
modern_techniques.description: Make traffic look like legitimate protocols (HTTPS, DNS, etc.)

modern_techniques.name: Traffic Padding
modern_techniques.description: Add random padding to packets to change size patterns

modern_techniques.name: Timing Obfuscation
modern_techniques.description: Randomize packet timing to break flow analysis

modern_techniques.name: Fake Traffic Injection
modern_techniques.description: Inject artificial traffic to mask real patterns

modern_techniques.name: Domain Fronting
modern_techniques.description: Hide real destination behind legitimate CDN

modern_techniques.name: Stealth Protocols
modern_techniques.description: Custom protocols designed to evade detection

recommendations: Implement multiple transport protocols with different fingerprints
Add pluggable transport architecture like Tor's PT spec
Use TLS 1.3 wrapping for HTTPS-like appearance
Implement traffic padding and timing randomization
Add fake traffic generator with realistic patterns

# Текущая структура проекта cellframe-srv-vpn-client

## Обзор
Проект `cellframe-srv-vpn-client` - это автономный консольный VPN клиент для сети Cellframe.

## Структура директорий

### Корневые файлы
- `CMakeLists.txt` - Основная сборка проекта
- `README.md` - Документация проекта
- `slc` - CLI скрипт СЛК системы
- `.context/` - СЛК система контекста

### Директория `source/` - Исходный код

#### `source/cli/` - CLI утилита vpn-ctl
**Назначение**: Управление VPN клиентом через командную строку
- `include/vpn_ctl.h` - Заголовочный файл CLI
- `vpn_ctl.c` - Реализация CLI команд

#### `source/clientd/` - Демон vpn-clientd
**Назначение**: Фоновый сервис VPN клиента
- `include/vpn_client_network.h` - Управление сетью
- `main.c` - Точка входа демона
- `vpn_clientd_main.c` - Основная логика демона
- `vpn_clientd_service.c` - Сервисная логика
- `vpn_clientd_service.h` - Заголовок сервиса

#### `source/common/` - Общий код
**Назначение**: Код используемый и CLI и демоном
- `include/vpn_protocol.h` - Определения протокола
- `vpn_protocol.c` - Реализация протокола

### Директория `dist/` - Файлы дистрибутива

#### `dist/common/` - Общие файлы
- `docs/vpn-clientd.8` - Man-страница демона
- `docs/vpn-ctl.1` - Man-страница CLI
- `examples/` - Примеры использования

#### `dist/linux/` - Linux специфичные файлы
- `common/systemd/cellframe-vpn-clientd.service` - systemd unit
- `debian/` - Debian packaging (control, postinst, postrm, prerm)
- `rpm/` - RPM packaging

#### `dist/macos/` - macOS специфичные файлы
- `launchd/com.cellframe.vpn-clientd.plist` - launchd plist

#### `dist/windows/` - Windows специфичные файлы

### Директория `etc/` - Конфигурация
- `networks.cfg.example` - Пример конфигурации сетей

### Директория `tests/` - Тестирование
- `e2e/` - End-to-end тесты
- `functional/` - Функциональные тесты
- `integration/` - Интеграционные тесты
- `performance/` - Тесты производительности
- `unit/` - Юнит-тесты
- `run.sh` - Скрипт запуска тестов
- `stage-env` - Тестовое окружение (симлинк)
- `stage-env.cfg` - Конфигурация окружения

### Директория `cellframe-node/` - Cellframe Node (submodule)
**Содержит**: Полную копию cellframe-node с SDK
- `cellframe-sdk/` - Cellframe SDK с VPN модулями
- `dap-sdk/` - DAP SDK

### Зависимости от SDK

#### Код VPN клиента в SDK (требует переноса)
**Путь**: `cellframe-node/cellframe-sdk/modules/service/vpn/client/`

**Файлы (20 файлов C + заголовки)**:
1. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_auto.c` - Автоподключение
2. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_backup.c` - Резервное копирование
3. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_cli.c` - CLI интерфейс
4. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_connectivity_test.c` - Тесты связи
5. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_ipc.c` - IPC коммуникация
6. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_multihop_tx.c` - Multi-hop транзакции
7. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_network.c` - Управление сетью
8. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_payment.c` - Платежи
9. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_protocol_probe.c` - Проверка протокола
10. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_protocol.c` - Протокол клиента
11. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_receipt.c` - Квитанции
12. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_service.c` - Сервис клиента
13. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_state_handlers.c` - Обработчики состояний
14. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_state_payment.c` - Состояние платежей
15. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_state_tunnel.c` - Состояние туннеля
16. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_state.c` - Управление состоянием
17. `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_uplink_pool.c` - Пул аплинков
18. `dap_vpn_client_balance_monitor.c` - Мониторинг баланса
19. `dap_vpn_client_network_registry.c` - Реестр сетей
20. `dap_vpn_client_wallet.c` - Кошелёк

**Платформо-зависимые файлы**:
- `platform/linux/network.c`, `platform.c`
- `platform/darwin/network.c`, `platform.c`
- `platform/bsd/network.c`, `platform.c`
- `platform/windows/network.c`, `platform.c`
- `platform/unix/network_common.c`, `network_common.h`

#### Код который ОСТАЁТСЯ в SDK
**Путь**: `cellframe-node/cellframe-sdk/modules/service/vpn/`

**VPN сервер (останется в SDK)**:
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn.c` - Основной сервис
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_addr_pool.c` - Пул адресов
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_callbacks.c` - Коллбэки
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_cmd.c` - Команды
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_common.c` - Общий код
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_limits.c` - Лимиты
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_multihop.c` - Multi-hop сервер
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_session.c` - Сессии
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_stream.c` - Стримы
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_traffic.c` - Трафик
- `dap_chain_net_srv_vpn_tun.c` - TUN устройство сервера

**Общий код (останется в SDK)**:
- `dap_chain_net_vpn_client.c` - Общая логика клиента/сервера
- `dap_chain_net_vpn_client_tun.c` - TUN клиента (общий)
- `tun/` - TUN device библиотека (общая)

## Текущая система сборки

### CMakeLists.txt
```cmake
# Использует файлы из SDK через симлинки
set(CELLFRAME_SDK_DIR "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/../cellframe-sdk")

# Подключает заголовки SDK
include_directories(
    ${CELLFRAME_SDK_DIR}/modules/service/vpn/client
    ${CELLFRAME_SDK_DIR}/dap-sdk/...
)

# Компилирует файлы из SDK
set(VPN_CLIENT_SDK_SOURCES
    ${CELLFRAME_SDK_DIR}/modules/service/vpn/client/dap_chain_net_vpn_client_protocol.c
    ${CELLFRAME_SDK_DIR}/modules/service/vpn/client/...
)
```

## Проблемы текущей архитектуры

1. **Зависимость от SDK**: Весь код клиента находится в SDK
2. **Дублирование**: Нужно симлинкать или копировать SDK
3. **Сложность сборки**: Требуется весь cellframe-sdk для сборки клиента
4. **Смешение ответственности**: Клиент и сервер в одном модуле SDK
5. **Зависимости**: Клиент тянет весь SDK включая ненужные модули

## Желаемая архитектура

### Принцип разделения
- **cellframe-srv-vpn-client**: Весь клиентский код
- **cellframe-sdk**: Только общие механизмы + серверный код

### Что переносим
- Все файлы из `cellframe-sdk/modules/service/vpn/client/`
- Клиентские части общих файлов
- Платформо-зависимый код клиента

### Что остаётся в SDK
- VPN сервер
- Общие механизмы (TUN device)
- Серверные части
- Базовые протоколы

# План переноса VPN клиента из SDK в cellframe-srv-vpn-client

## Общая стратегия

### Принципы
1. **Минимальные изменения SDK**: В SDK остаются только общие механизмы и серверный код
2. **Автономность клиента**: Клиент должен собираться независимо
3. **Обратная совместимость**: API и ABI совместимость
4. **Чистая архитектура**: Разделение ответственности

### 3.1 Новый CMakeLists.txt

```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.10)
project(cellframe-vpn-client VERSION 1.0.0 LANGUAGES C)

set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)

# =============================================================================
# Dependencies
# =============================================================================

# DAP SDK (минимальные модули)
find_library(DAP_CORE dap_core REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_CRYPTO dap_crypto REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_IO dap_io REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_CLIENT dap_client REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_STREAM dap_stream REQUIRED)

# Cellframe SDK (только общие механизмы)
find_library(DAP_CHAIN_COMMON dap_chain_common REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_CHAIN_NET dap_chain_net REQUIRED)
find_library(DAP_NET_TUN dap_net_tun REQUIRED)  # TUN device library

# JSON-C
pkg_check_modules(JSON_C REQUIRED json-c)

# =============================================================================
# VPN Client Library
# =============================================================================

set(VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES
    source/client/core/vpn_client_core.c
    source/client/auto/vpn_client_auto.c
    source/client/backup/vpn_client_backup.c
    source/client/connectivity/vpn_client_connectivity.c
    source/client/ipc/vpn_client_ipc.c
    source/client/multihop/vpn_client_multihop.c
    source/client/network/vpn_client_network.c
    source/client/payment/vpn_client_payment.c
    source/client/protocol/vpn_client_protocol.c
    source/client/protocol/vpn_client_protocol_probe.c
    source/client/receipt/vpn_client_receipt.c
    source/client/service/vpn_client_service.c
    source/client/state/vpn_client_state.c
    source/client/state/vpn_client_state_handlers.c
    source/client/state/vpn_client_state_payment.c
    source/client/state/vpn_client_state_tunnel.c
    source/client/uplink/vpn_client_uplink.c
    source/client/balance/vpn_client_balance.c
    source/client/registry/vpn_client_registry.c
    source/client/wallet/vpn_client_wallet.c
)

# Platform-specific sources
if(CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME STREQUAL "Linux")
    list(APPEND VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES
        source/client/platform/unix/network_common.c
        source/client/platform/linux/network.c
        source/client/platform/linux/platform.c
    )
elseif(CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME STREQUAL "Darwin")
    list(APPEND VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES
        source/client/platform/unix/network_common.c
        source/client/platform/darwin/network.c
        source/client/platform/darwin/platform.c
    )
elseif(CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME STREQUAL "Windows")
    list(APPEND VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES
        source/client/platform/windows/network.c
        source/client/platform/windows/platform.c
    )
elseif(CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME MATCHES "BSD")
    list(APPEND VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES
        source/client/platform/unix/network_common.c
        source/client/platform/bsd/network.c
        source/client/platform/bsd/platform.c
    )
endif()

# VPN Client library
add_library(vpn_client STATIC ${VPN_CLIENT_SOURCES})

target_include_directories(vpn_client PUBLIC
    source/client/include
    source/common/include
)

target_link_libraries(vpn_client
    ${DAP_CORE}
    ${DAP_CRYPTO}
    ${DAP_IO}
    ${DAP_CLIENT}
    ${DAP_STREAM}
    ${DAP_CHAIN_COMMON}
    ${DAP_CHAIN_NET}
    ${DAP_NET_TUN}
    ${JSON_C_LIBRARIES}
)

# =============================================================================
# VPN Client Daemon (vpn-clientd)
# =============================================================================

add_executable(vpn-clientd
    source/clientd/main.c
    source/clientd/vpn_clientd_main.c
    source/clientd/vpn_clientd_service.c
)

target_link_libraries(vpn-clientd
    vpn_client
    ${DAP_CORE}
    ${JSON_C_LIBRARIES}
)

# =============================================================================
# VPN Control CLI (vpn-ctl)
# =============================================================================

add_executable(vpn-ctl
    source/cli/vpn_ctl.c
)

target_link_libraries(vpn-ctl
    vpn_client
    ${DAP_CORE}
    ${JSON_C_LIBRARIES}
)

# =============================================================================
# Installation
# =============================================================================

install(TARGETS vpn-clientd vpn-ctl
    RUNTIME DESTINATION bin
)

install(FILES source/client/include/vpn_client.h
    DESTINATION include/cellframe-vpn-client
)

# =============================================================================
# Tests
# =============================================================================

enable_testing()

add_subdirectory(tests/unit)
```

progress_log.summary: Generic Handshake Protocol - TLV format specification

details.design_scope: Transport-agnostic handshake protocol using Type-Length-Value encoding

current_problems: HTTP query string binding makes non-HTTP transports awkward
JSON response inefficient for binary data (base64 overhead ~33%)
Hard to implement in constrained environments (UDP, embedded)
motivation.solution: Binary TLV protocol, transport-agnostic, efficient, extensible

tlv_encoding.description: Type-Length-Value format for all handshake messages

length.notes: Length does NOT include Type and Length fields themselves

handshake_request.name: DSHP_MSG_HANDSHAKE_REQUEST
handshake_request.description: Initial handshake request with encryption parameters

alice_pub_key.description: Client's public key for key exchange

example_packet.description: Magic + Version 1.0 + HANDSHAKE_REQUEST + AES256 + RLWE + 1024-byte key + 32-byte block + alice pubkey

handshake_response.name: DSHP_MSG_HANDSHAKE_RESPONSE
handshake_response.description: Server's handshake response with session ID and public key

bob_pub_key.description: Server public key for key exchange

session_create_request.name: DSHP_MSG_SESSION_CREATE
session_create_request.description: Request to create streaming session with channels
session_create_request.encryption: Encrypted with shared key from handshake

encoding_rules.strings: UTF-8, NOT null-terminated (length from TLV Length field)
encoding_rules.nested_tlv: Value can contain nested TLV structures

parsing_rules.unknown_types: MUST skip unknown Type IDs using Length field
parsing_rules.duplicate_types: Last occurrence wins (or reject as error)
parsing_rules.order: Fields MAY appear in any order
parsing_rules.extension: Future versions can add new Type IDs without breaking old parsers

advantages.transport_agnostic: Works over HTTP, UDP, WebSocket, TLS, DNS, any binary transport
advantages.efficient: Binary encoding, ~70% smaller than JSON for typical handshake
advantages.extensible: New Type IDs can be added without breaking old implementations
advantages.fast_parsing: Simple state machine, no text parsing overhead
advantages.obfuscation_friendly: Binary format easier to obfuscate than HTTP headers
advantages.embedded_friendly: Low memory footprint, no JSON parser needed

backward_compatibility.strategy: HTTP transport adapter translates between HTTP and TLV
backward_compatibility.http_to_tlv: Parse HTTP query params + JSON body → construct TLV message
backward_compatibility.tlv_to_http: Parse TLV message → construct HTTP request/response
backward_compatibility.detection: Server detects protocol by first bytes: 0x0100 (magic) = TLV, else HTTP

next_steps: Create dap_stream_handshake.h header with TLV type definitions
Update HTTP adapter to translate between HTTP and TLV
Add TLV support to client stage machine

obfuscate_send.signature: int (*obfuscate_send)(dap_stream_obfuscation_t *a_obfs, const void *a_plain_data, size_t a_plain_size, void **a_obfs_data_out, size_t *a_obfs_size_out)
obfuscate_send.returns: 0 on success with obfuscated data in a_obfs_data_out

deobfuscate_recv.notes: Caller must free a_plain_data_out. May return 0 with NULL if dummy traffic.

generate_dummy_traffic.description: Generate fake traffic packet for injection

get_padding_params.description: Query padding parameters for given plaintext size

get_timing_delay.description: Get artificial delay for next packet (microseconds)
get_timing_delay.notes: Used for timing obfuscation to disrupt traffic analysis

fields: uint64_t total_sent_bytes - for traffic shape tracking
float dummy_traffic_ratio - ratio of dummy to real traffic (0.0-1.0)
uint64_t dummy_interval_ms - interval for dummy packets

random_padding.description: Add random padding to blur packet sizes
random_padding.implementation: Append random bytes, prepend padding length as uint16_t

timing_obfuscation.implementation: Schedule delayed send with random µs offset

dummy_traffic_injection.description: Inject fake packets to alter traffic patterns
dummy_traffic_injection.implementation: Background scheduler sends dummy packets at random intervals
dummy_traffic_injection.detection: Receiver detects DUMMY type, discards silently
dummy_traffic_injection.benefits: Defeats flow correlation, hides real traffic patterns

https_mimicry.implementation: Wrap obfuscated data in fake TLS 1.3 records
https_mimicry.benefits: Looks like HTTPS to shallow DPI

polymorphic_encoding.implementation: Rotate between padding strategies every N packets

fields: dap_list_t *active_streams - streams eligible for dummy traffic
uint64_t next_injection_time - next scheduled dummy packet

operation.on_timer: For each active stream, probabilistically inject dummy packet

steps: 3. If obfuscation enabled: call obfs->ops->obfuscate_send(data, size, &obfs_data, &obfs_size)
4. If timing delay: schedule send after obfs->ops->get_timing_delay() µs
3. If obfuscation enabled: call obfs->ops->deobfuscate_recv(obfs_data, obfs_size, &plain_data, &plain_size)
4. If plain_data == NULL: packet was dummy traffic, discard silently
5. If plain_data != NULL: pass plain_data to stream processor

protocol_fingerprinting.effectiveness: Very High - looks like legitimate protocol

ml_based_dpi.effectiveness: Extreme - constantly changing patterns defeat classifiers

pluggable_architecture.benefit: Users can choose overhead vs. DPI resistance trade-off

subphase_6_8_details.title: Multi-threaded TUN Device Management
subphase_6_8_details.description: Implement efficient multi-threaded I/O with TUN/TAP devices, utilizing multiple threads based on hardware queue count for optimal throughput

rationale.performance: Modern NICs have multiple hardware queues (typically 2-16), single-threaded I/O becomes bottleneck
rationale.throughput: Multi-threaded approach can saturate Gigabit+ connections
rationale.latency: Parallel processing reduces packet processing latency

worker_assignment.description: Use existing dap_worker threads, assign TUN sockets to them via dap_events_socket
worker_assignment.strategy: Distribute TUN device FDs across existing workers instead of creating dedicated threads
worker_assignment.thread_count: Use dap_get_cpu_count() to match existing worker pool size

detection.linux: Read /sys/class/net/<iface>/queues/ directory (count rx-* and tx-* subdirectories)
detection.macos: sysctl net.link.generic.system.hwcksum_tx_queues or default to CPU count
detection.windows: GetAdapterInfo() or default to CPU count

integration.approach: Register TUN FDs as dap_events_socket objects
integration.assignment: Use dap_worker_add_events_socket() to assign each TUN FD to a specific worker
integration.callbacks: Set read/write callbacks on dap_events_socket for packet processing

subphase_6_10_details.title: Multi-Hop VPN with Parallel Tunnels & Conditional Payment Receipts
subphase_6_10_details.description: Implement advanced multi-hop routing with support for multiple simultaneous tunnels per hop, traffic balancing, and complex payment verification with conditional transaction receipts from all intermediate nodes

rationale.security: Multi-hop provides layered encryption and anonymity (onion-routing style)
rationale.performance: Parallel tunnels per hop enable load balancing and higher aggregate throughput
rationale.flexibility: Conditional TSD allows server-side control of tunnel multiplicity
rationale.payment_transparency: Receipt verification ensures all hops are paid and operational

responsibilities: Parse multi-hop route specification from transaction TSD
Establish multiple tunnels to first hop (if allowed by TSD)
Manage layered encryption (encrypt for hop N, then N-1, ..., then hop 1)
Balance traffic across parallel tunnels
Collect and verify payment receipts from all hops
Monitor health of all tunnels and hops
Handle failover on tunnel/hop failure
Decrypt outer layer, forward to next hop
Maintain multiple tunnels to next hop (if requested)
Issue conditional payment receipts (checks) on exit transaction
Track data usage per client session
Forward receipts from downstream hops to client
Handle nested stream creation (stream-in-stream)
Decrypt final layer, route to Internet
Issue payment receipt directly to client
Track data usage for billing
Provide actual Internet connectivity

data_flow.outbound: Client → [Tunnel 1..N to Hop 1] → [Tunnel 1..N to Hop 2] → ... → [Exit Node] → Internet
data_flow.inbound: Internet → Exit Node → ... → Hop 2 → Hop 1 → Client

subphase_6_11_details.title: VPN Kill Switch - Traffic Leak Prevention
subphase_6_11_details.description: Implement automatic traffic blocking when VPN is disconnected or during reconnection to prevent accidental data leaks. Enabled by default, configurable via CLI/config.

rationale.security_requirement: Critical security feature to prevent traffic leaks during VPN failures
use_cases: VPN connection drops → all traffic blocked until reconnected
Reconnection in progress → traffic blocked during transition
Uplink switching → traffic blocked during handover
Daemon crash → system firewall rules persist, blocking traffic
rationale.threat_model: Prevent IP/DNS leaks, protect user identity even during VPN failures

default_behavior.description: Kill switch ALWAYS enabled by default for maximum security
default_behavior.override: User can explicitly disable via --no-kill-switch flag or config option

blocking_conditions: VPN_STATE_VERIFYING_CONNECTIVITY → block all traffic (verification in progress)
VPN_STATE_ROUTING_SETUP → block all traffic (routing table update in progress)
allowed_conditions: VPN_STATE_CONNECTED → allow all traffic through VPN tunnel
Kill switch disabled in config → allow all traffic (user override)

exceptions.vpn_server_ips: Always allow direct connection to VPN server IPs (for establishing connection)
exceptions.dns_resolution: Allow DNS to VPN servers ONLY (for resolving server hostnames)
exceptions.dhcp: Always allow DHCP (UDP 67/68) for network configuration

exception_rules: ALLOW output to VPN server IPs (from backup.vpn_server_addresses)
ALLOW DNS queries to VPN server IPs port 53
vpn_active_rules: ALLOW all traffic through VPN interface (tun0/utun0/wintun)
DROP all traffic through physical interfaces (except VPN server IPs)

hook_points.entry_action: Install kill switch rules BEFORE attempting connection
hook_points.rationale: Block traffic before VPN is established

hook_points.entry_action: Keep kill switch active, add VPN server exception rules
Update kill switch to allow traffic through VPN interface
hook_points.rationale: VPN active, allow traffic through tunnel

hook_points.entry_action: Revert to full block (DROP all traffic)
hook_points.rationale: Connection lost, block everything until reconnected

hook_points.entry_action: Keep kill switch active until DISCONNECTED
Remove kill switch rules if kill_switch.persist_on_disconnect = false
hook_points.exit_action: Install kill switch rules if connecting again
hook_points.rationale: Optional: remove rules on clean disconnect

3. Expected: Ping fails (kill switch blocks traffic)

privilege_escalation.issue: Firewall rule modification requires root/admin privileges
privilege_escalation.mitigation_linux: Use polkit for privilege escalation, or run daemon as root with dropped privileges
privilege_escalation.mitigation_macos: Use SMJobBless for privileged helper, or require sudo
privilege_escalation.mitigation_windows: Require running as Administrator, or use Windows Service with LocalSystem

rule_conflicts.issue: Kill switch rules may conflict with existing firewall rules
rule_conflicts.mitigation: Use high priority/weight for kill switch rules, use dedicated chains/anchors/filters

dns_leaks.issue: DNS queries may leak before VPN connected
dns_leaks.mitigation: Block DNS to all except VPN server IPs in kill switch rules

ipv6_leaks.issue: IPv6 traffic may bypass IPv4 kill switch
ipv6_leaks.mitigation: Apply kill switch rules to both IPv4 and IPv6 (ip6tables, pf inet6, WFP IPv6 layer)

webrtc_leaks.issue: WebRTC may expose real IP even with VPN
webrtc_leaks.mitigation: Kill switch blocks all non-VPN traffic, preventing WebRTC leaks at network layer

connectivity_loss.mitigation: Clear documentation, CLI flag to disable, emergency disable mechanism (remove firewall rules manually)

firewall_persistence.risk: Firewall rules may persist after daemon crash, blocking all traffic
firewall_persistence.mitigation: On Linux/macOS: daemon should remove rules on clean exit; Windows: WFP filters auto-managed by system

platform_specific_bugs.risk: Firewall APIs may behave differently across platforms
